The largest upward movement occurred on 2026-03-03, when the stock rose by 3.8753% to close at 270.99 on a volume of 6,206,600 shares, coinciding with multiple news items including anticipation of Q1 FY2026 earnings results scheduled for March 12, 2026, and reports of strategic moves related to AI, which may have contributed to the positive price action [1][3]. Conversely, the largest downward day was on 2026-02-03, with a decline of -7.3113% closing at 271.93 on a notably high volume of 8,324,900 shares, though no specific driver from the provided data directly aligns with this date. A high volume day on 2025-12-11 saw a 2.1275% price increase to 350.43 with 10,158,700 shares traded, reflecting heightened market activity. Other notable news around early March includes mixed analyst sentiment such as Piper Sandler maintaining a neutral stance [2], as well as institutional trading activity with some funds purchasing shares and others trimming positions [8][10][11]. Additionally, regulatory and legal concerns were reported, including potential monetary costs related to an FTC investigation and a securities class action dismissal, which may have influenced market perceptions [15][16]. Overall, the stock's performance and volatility were shaped by a combination of earnings anticipation, strategic developments, institutional trading, and regulatory news.
